input.background: A South African study compared whether participating in a 5-year follow-up interview was associated with having medical aid (similar to health insurance).
input.question: Calculate the prevalence of medical aid in the group that was followed up and the group that was lost to follow up. Describe the relationship between follow up and having medical aid.

output.answer: Among the 416 children who were followed-up, 46(11.1%) had medical aid. In contrast, 195 of 1174(16.6%) who were not followed-up had medical aid. Therefore, there is a negative association between follow-up and having medical aid.

Follow-up Status.description: Indicates whether a participant was followed up or lost to follow-up.

Medical Aid Status.description: Indicates whether a participant has medical aid (health insurance) or not.

Follow-up + with Medical Aid.description: Number of participants who were followed up and have medical aid.

Follow-up + without Medical Aid.description: Number of participants who were followed up and do not have medical aid.

Follow-up - with Medical Aid.description: Number of participants who were lost to follow-up and have medical aid.

Follow-up - without Medical Aid.description: Number of participants who were lost to follow-up and do not have medical aid.

meta_info.caption: Is participating in a follow-up survey associated with having medical aid?
